title: ipv6 support for portmap
body:
hi,

I try to use podman on a centos 8 host. The host has only Ipv6. So I want portmap a container with ipv6. 
I could not find any option on podman create to make it listen on ipv6

comments.body: Are you running as root, or rootless?

podman container create  -p 9115:9115 blackbox_exporter
podman container start $container_id

After this it  is only listen on ipv4
Again - are you running Podman as root, or as a normal user?
I was testing as root sorry
@ruben-herold @mheon What is going on with this one?
Sorry, this slipped through my radar. To listen on a port via IPv6, `podman run -p [::]:8080:80 ...` should forward port 80 in the container to port 8080 on all IPv6 addresses on the host

Listening on an IPv6 port works but the container doesn't receive any request:
```
root@podman:~# podman run -d -p [::]:8080:80 nginx
b97753fa31d88938352f90d6818ca1c79a798ec915e984eeda981c998b5ed9dd
root@podman:~# lsof -i:8080
COMMAND  PID USER   FD   TYPE DEVICE SIZE/OFF NODE NAME
conmon  9817 root    5u  IPv6  53231      0t0  TCP *:http-alt (LISTEN)
root@podman:~# curl -m 10 [::1]:8080
curl: (28) Operation timed out after 10000 milliseconds with 0 bytes received
```

Here is the result in IPv4:
```
root@podman:~# podman run -d -p 8080:80 nginx
9f6f754fe0003760c9d8b537e908cf4be1c1d1a618c6d4c7471054b610c2c416
root@podman:~# lsof -i:8080
COMMAND  PID USER   FD   TYPE DEVICE SIZE/OFF NODE NAME
conmon  9513 root    5u  IPv4  52042      0t0  TCP *:http-alt (LISTEN)
root@podman:~# curl -I 127.0.0.1:8080
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Server: nginx/1.19.3
```

podman version 2.1.1 and it is running as root

Any idea what podman version first implements this ipv6 solution "podman run -p [::]:8080:80 ..."? This obviously doesn't work in RHEL 8.2.  **Podman version 1.9.3**

``
 podman run -p [::]:80:80 -p 80:80 --name web -d docker.io/library/nginx:latest 
``

**output:** Error: cannot resolve the TCP address: address :::80: too many colons in address

I think I'm having the same issue @unixfox mentioned [above](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7606#issuecomment-708435854). Below is the response I got when using curl. **IP address is modified in the sample data below.** ``` [rootuser ~]# curl -6 -v -k -g http://[::12] * Rebuilt URL to: http://[::12]/ * Trying ::12... * TCP_NODELAY set * Connected to ::12 (::12) port 80 (#0) > GET / HTTP/1.1 > Host: [::12] > User-Agent: curl/7.61.1 > Accept: */* > ``` IPv6 connection above hangs -- response is timeout. ``` [rootuser ~]# curl -4 -k -v http://1.1.1.1 * Rebuilt URL to: http://1.1.1.1 * Trying 1.1.1.1... * TCP_NODELAY set * Connected to 1.1.1.1 (1.1.1.1) port 80 (#0) > GET / HTTP/1.1 > Host: 1.1.1.1 > User-Agent: curl/7.61.1 > Accept: */* > < HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found < Server: nginx < Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8 < Content-Length: 340 < Connection: close < 404 - Not Found

404 - Not Found

* Closing connection 0 ``` IPv4 works fine
Sounds to me like a duplicate of #7415
@ericzolf Yeah I think it's the same issue.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing since this is a duplicate of #7415
